title: Measure Would Restore Public Trust and Confidence in Elections – and Fulfils Condition Imposed by President Trump

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California voters do not trust in elections due to perceived issues in election integrity.",
    "The California Voter ID and Election Integrity Act of 2025 aims to enhance election integrity in California.",
    "The bill proposes a requirement for government-issued ID to vote in person and on mail-in ballots.",
    "The bill mandates verification of citizenship before adding individuals to voter rolls.",
    "Verification of existing voter registrations must be proven by appropriate identification documentation, not self-attestation.",
    "The bill requires the state auditor to conduct a random sample of voter lists to ensure at least 98% accuracy.",
    "Counties that fail to maintain accurate voter lists may not mail ballots automatically to everyone on their voter list for the election cycle.",
    "The bill proposes penalties for counties that do not count all mail-in ballots within 72 hours of the election.",
    "The state auditor is required to audit the signature reviews of county election offices for proper vetting of signatures.",
    "Delays in counting and reporting electoral results have exposed weaknesses in the election process and undermined public trust."
  ]
}
```
